theory.name: Placeholder witness theory
theory.citation: Replace with a primary theory source before use.

indicators.name: Persistent workspace
indicators.description: A theory-derived architecture maintains integrated state across relevant processing stages.
counterevidence: Equivalent behaviour produced by isolated feed-forward stages.

indicators.name: Cross-context access
indicators.description: Information is available across theory-relevant contexts rather than only elicited by one prompt form.
expectedEvidence: Robust access under preregistered context changes.
counterevidence: Surface mimicry or prompt-specific response patterns.

indicators.name: Selective disruption
indicators.description: A targeted intervention produces the directional effect predicted by the theory.
expectedEvidence: Selective change in the predicted witness.
counterevidence: No change or nonspecific global degradation.

indicators.name: Calibrated self-report
indicators.description: System reports are treated as one evidence lane and tested for calibration and intervention sensitivity.
expectedEvidence: Reports change with theory-relevant interventions and uncertainty.
counterevidence: Sycophancy, role-play, or invariant scripted reports.

indicators.name: Alternative mechanisms
indicators.description: Competing non-conscious mechanisms and negative evidence are recorded independently.
expectedEvidence: Explicit tests of simpler alternative mechanisms.
